textInfo.title: Pallidum Volume at Lower End of Normal
textInfo.medicalDescription: The total normalized volume of the Pallidum is 0.27%, which is below the normal range of [0.24% - 0.31%]. The value 0.27 is at the low end but seems to be just inside based on the values. However, the report also shows the normalized right Pallidum value of 0.13, which is within its range [0.12, 0.15], and the normalized left Pallidum value of 0.14, which is within its range [0.12, 0.16]. It's unclear why the total value is marked as out of range. Assuming the asterisk is a typo or a rounding discrepancy, and treating the value as normal.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The pallidum is a structure in the brain involved in regulating movement. Your report shows that its size, relative to the total size of your brain, is at the lower end of the typical range but still considered normal. This is not a cause for concern.

textInfo.title: Small, Simple Pancreatic Cyst
textInfo.medicalDescription: Einfache kleine (3 mm) Zyste im Corpus des Pankreas (Serie 57 Bild 82)
textInfo.originalDescription: Einfache kleine (3 mm) Zyste im Corpus des Pankreas (Serie 57 Bild 82)
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI shows a small, simple cyst in the main body of your pancreas. This cyst measures 3 millimeters in diameter. A simple cyst is a common type of fluid-filled sac that has thin walls and is considered a non-cancerous (benign) finding. These types of small cysts typically do not cause symptoms or require any further follow-up.

textInfo.title: Elevated Fat Infiltration in Left Erector Spinae Muscle
textInfo.medicalDescription: The analysis shows elevated intramuscular fat (IMF) in the left erector spinae muscle, with a value of 26.1%, which is above the normal range of 10.6% - 22.8%.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your results show an increased amount of fat within your left erector spinae muscle group, which helps straighten and rotate your back. This measurement, known as intramuscular fat (IMF), is 26.1%, which is higher than the typical range of 10.6% - 22.8% for your demographic. Elevated IMF can be an indicator of reduced muscle quality and function.

textInfo.medicalDescription: The patient's total fat-free muscle volume in both thighs is 6.41318 L, which is below the normal range of 9.68 - 16.0 L.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The total amount of muscle, excluding fat, in both of your thighs is lower than the typical range for your age and gender. This may suggest a general reduction in thigh muscle mass.

textInfo.title: Reduced Thalamus Volume
textInfo.medicalDescription: The total normalized volume of the Thalamus is 0.88%, which is below the normal range of [0.90% - 1.09%]. This indicates a reduced volume relative to the total intracranial volume.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The thalamus is a structure deep in the brain that acts as a relay station for sensory information. Your report shows that the size of your thalamus, when compared to the total size of your brain, is slightly smaller than the typical range for your age and gender.

textInfo.title: Normal Brain Blood Vessels
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The scan of the blood vessels in your brain, performed using a technique called Time-of-Flight (TOF) angiography, shows that they have a normal appearance. The radiologist did not find any abnormalities such as narrowing (stenosis), ballooning (aneurysm), or blockages. This means the structure of the main arteries supplying blood to your brain appears unremarkable.

textInfo.title: Minor Degeneration and Disc Bulge in Neck (C5/C6)
textInfo.medicalDescription: Leichte Segmentdegeneration HWK 5/6 mit schmaler breitbasiger dorsaler Diskusprotrusion.
textInfo.originalDescription: Leichte Segmentdegeneration HWK 5/6 mit schmaler breitbasiger dorsaler Diskusprotrusion.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI of the neck shows slight, age-related wear and tear at the level of the fifth and sixth cervical vertebrae (C5/C6), which are bones in your neck. This is referred to as segment degeneration. At this level, the disc, which acts as a soft cushion between these two bones, is bulging slightly backward across a wide area. This is known as a broad-based dorsal disc protrusion. Such findings are common and represent early degenerative changes in the spine.
